content.problemStatement:
Chef Dengklek will open a new restaurant in the city. The restaurant will be open for N days. He can cook M different types of dish. He would like to cook a single dish every day, such that for the entire N days, he only cook at most K distinct types of dishes.

In how many ways can he do that?
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of a single line consisting of three integers N, M, K.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line consisting of the number of different ways he can cook for the entire N days, modulo 1000000007.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, there is only one way to cook for one day with one type of dish. For the second test case, there are four ways to cook for two days with two types of dishes: (1,1), (1,2), (2,1), (2,2). For the third test case, there are 45 ways to cook for four days with two types of dishes. For the fourth test case, there are 5887 ways to cook for five days with three types of dishes.

For the first test case, there are 36 ways to cook for three days with two types of dishes out of four. For the second test case, there are 1812 ways to cook for five days with three types of dishes out of six.
For this test case, there are 100000 ways to cook for ten days with five types of dishes out of ten.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved using combinatorics and modular arithmetic. We need to calculate the number of ways to choose K dishes out of M and then calculate the number of ways to arrange these K dishes in N days. This can be done using the formula for combinations and the formula for modular exponentiation.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * K) per test case, O(T * K) total. The time complexity is linear with respect to the number of test cases and the number of dishes.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — the space complexity is constant as we only use a fixed amount of space to store the input and the output.
